texto: Morality can provide at most only a severely limited and insufficient answer to the question of how a person should live.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Harry G. Frankfurt sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “Morality can provide at most only a severely limited and insufficient answer to the question of h…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, frankfurt, love, útil em momentos de love e philosophy.

texto: Someone who lies and someone who tells the truth are playing on opposite sides, so to speak, in the same game. Each responds to the facts as he understands them, although the response of the one is guided by the authority of the truth, while the response of the other defies that authority and refuses to meet its demands. The bullshitter ignores these demands altogether. He does not reject the authority of the truth, as the liar does, and oppose himself to it. He pays no attention to it at all. By virtue of this, bullshit is a greater enemy of the truth than lies are.
explicacao: Citação de Harry G. Frankfurt sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Someone who lies and someone who tells the truth are playing on opposite sides, so to speak, in t…” articula reflexao, bullshit, lie em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como lie e verdade.

texto: Our natures are, indeed, elusively insubstantial—notoriously less stable and less inherent than the nature of other things. And insofar as this is the case, sincerity itself is bullshit.
explicacao: Citação de Harry G. Frankfurt sobre temas ligados a autoestima. “Our natures are, indeed, elusively insubstantial—notoriously less stable and less inherent than t…” articula autoestima, 2005, bullshit em registro de autoestima, com relevância para contextos como bullshit e philosophy.
